reviews.review_title: Nice Mix of Defense and Shooting
reviews.review_description: I don't really enjoy the Tower Defense game. It's because I hate the frustration of just defense. However, this is different. If properly deployed according to the opponent's movement, you can experience a pleasant destruction effect, and you can directly attack as a turret gunner. It is more difficult to fire a manual aiming compared to automatic response, but there is an attack power bonus, so you can fully enjoy the exhilaration of the attackers at the same time.

developer_response.body:
I truly want to thank every player who has supported and encouraged this game.

When I first started developing it, my goal was to create a tower defense experience that felt more active and exciting, not just passive defense. I wanted players to enjoy both strategic deployment and the thrill of directly fighting back through shooting mechanics. Seeing players spend time learning the gameplay, sharing feedback, and enjoying the combat experience has been incredibly motivating for me.

Reading comments about the satisfying mix of defense and shooting, or how rewarding manual aiming feels compared to automatic attacks, genuinely means a lot. Those reactions remind me why I started this project in the first place.

I’ll continue improving the game, adding more content, and creating even more exciting moments for players to enjoy.

Thank you again for all your support.
Every comment, every play session, and every bit of encouragement keeps me moving forward.

reviews.review_description: First time playing a game like this. I’ve watched my son play a similar game and I found it interesting. The first look is good the layout is very easy to use. The setting have the right options to play around with. Game play is was much better after turning off the tunnel view. Easy to work out how things work and how to play. The x2 is a good feature. It is addictive when you get into it. The more you do the better the tower weapons you get. You can also upgrade them as you go. Best touch is that you can actually become the tower and fire it yourself. Overall a very good game.

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing the game! I’m really glad you had fun.

reviews.review_description: Some tower defense games I find stressful, this one is relaxing because it is mostly about the setup, which makes it more strategic rather than how fast you can mash buttons. The addition of being able to control one of the turrets adds something extra. If you like enjoyable setup style defense games, this should be up your alley.

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing the game! I’m really glad you had fun.

reviews.review_description:
This game was a surprise! It's basically a tower defense with 3D graphics, many towers to unlock and to choose, you collect money, buy towers or upgrade them, there are many waves of enemies, and you must take em down. What's the Surprise? Point at the tower, aim at the shooting sign, and you take the direct control of the tower! Yes, you take down the spaceship shooting directly from the towers. POWERRRRRR!!!!!!!!!
Very very funny game, I got some delays while playing but not sure if they came from the Meta Quest 2 or the game, and they did not affect at all the gameplay!!!! Totally worth your time!!!! Enjoy!!!!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for trying out the game! I'm thrilled that you enjoyed it.

reviews.review_description:
I love the fact that you can choose to embody one of the turrets and shoot in 1st person. It adds a new dimension and a degree of immersion. I would love to have more variety of stages though. Keep the good work developing the game. 
Also you can't beat the price.

developer_response.body: I’m thrilled you enjoyed the game! Thank you for playing.

reviews.review_title: A refreshing mix of strategy and action
reviews.review_description:
Building defenses and jumping into manual control makes every battle feel intense and rewarding. You’re not just placing towers and waiting. you’re constantly reacting, adjusting, and stepping in when things get overwhelming. The pacing feels natural, and each wave keeps you engaged without becoming frustrating.

And unlocking new loadouts and experimenting with different setups makes the game feel fresh. It’s satisfying to slowly improve your strategy over time. 

Had a lot of fun with this. Definitely worth trying= D

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing the game! I’m really glad you had fun. Feel free to share any questions or feedback you might have.

reviews.review_description:
This is a typical  tower defense style game, as far  as I can tell in my first impressions. I played  until my headset died, and I have a busy week ahead of me, but wanted to get a review in before I get too  distracted.

I don't play Tower defense  too often, but when I do, this is what I am looking for. It runs perfectly, it is simple  to pick up and play. It has leaderboards, requires strategy, looks clean and is easy to learn. This game supports smooth and snap turning, smooth locomotion and has adjustable vignetting. It might also have teleportation options, but I don't remember and my headset is dead right now (charging)

I am not going to pretend that it offers anything remarkably new, because  it doesn't so far but it is solid.  I have 4 weapon types at my disposal right now which vary from low damage rapid fire to slower fire high  damage and a damage over time style weapon, which checks all of the boxes I would expect. There are still a lot of levels ahead of me and more weapons to unlock, and I appear to be very low on the current leader boards (against the other people who have played this) so obviously I need to improve my strategy. 

I considered giving it a 4 star, because  it hasn't done anything exceptional yet,  but it does everything I  expect  it to and I don't want to be the one to ruin a perfect score, especially when I haven't run into any   problems and haven't  finished the game yet. I feel  this game is exactly what you think of when you want to play a Tower Defense game. The $9.99 price feels perfect for what you are getting.

developer_response.body:
Thank you very much for the review, I really appreciate it.

By the way, teleportation is available. You can activate it by pushing the right joystick forward. Also, every turret can be entered for manual aiming and firing, which gives you extra firepower. Using that at the right moment can also become part of your strategy.

Thanks again for taking the time to play the game, and good luck climbing to the top of the leaderboard!

reviews.review_description:
My girlfriend got mad at me, said i was gona play a few minutes and it turned into a few hours
Love the music in the game

developer_response.body: Thank you for checking out the game! I’m so glad you had a great time.

reviews.review_description: Strike Command offers a dynamic real-time strategy (RTS) experience with a welcome twist: actively taking control of a turret for 50% more firepower! The controls are intuitive, the combat is captivating, and the tactical depth keeps you coming back for more. An excellent title for fans of virtual reality real-time strategy.

developer_response.body: I’m thrilled you enjoyed the game! Thank you for playing. If you have any questions or ideas, feel free to let me know.

reviews.review_title: 🎯 Packed Tower Defense with a Twist
reviews.review_description:
Strike Command is a next-generation tower defense game that successfully blends action, strategy, and arcade gameplay.

One of its most interesting mechanics is the ability to “possess” a pawn from your defense line, allowing you to directly control it with a higher firing rate and longer shooting distance. At any moment you can jump back into strategy mode to build or upgrade turrets and continue defending your power core.

The game is engaging and well executed, offering 20 missions across different difficulty levels (easy, medium, and hard). As you progress, you gradually unlock multiple turret types, adding more strategic depth to each level.

The leaderboard system is based on how quickly you can defeat the waves of invading aliens, encouraging replayability and optimization.

With its colorful visuals, solid gameplay, and fun mechanics, Strike Command is a polished and enjoyable tower defense experience that’s definitely worth a try. 🚀👾

developer_response.body: I’m really happy you loved the game! Thanks for playing. If you have any suggestions or questions, I’d love to hear them.

reviews.review_title: Solid VR Tower Defense with Real Depth
reviews.review_description:
I’ve played Strike Command, and it really stands out as a well-made VR tower defense game.

What I liked most is that you’re not just passively watching the action—you can actively jump in and control the battle, which makes it much more immersive. The sense of scale in VR adds a fresh layer, making it feel great to look over the battlefield and plan your strategy.

The visuals are clean, and the interface is intuitive, so it’s easy to get into. Placing towers and timing your actions creates a satisfying gameplay loop that keeps you coming back.

It’s easy to pick up for casual play, but the more you dive into the strategy, the more rewarding it becomes. Definitely recommended for fans of VR tower defense games! 👍

developer_response.body: I’m really happy you loved the game! Thanks for playing.

reviews.review_title: Just One More Round… Famous Last Words
reviews.review_description:
I went into Strike Command planning to play a couple of stages. You know, a quick little tower defense snack before doing something responsible like… sleeping. Instead I kept telling myself “just one more round.” Next thing I know I’m staring at the credits after clearing all 20 stages. Oops. My “quick session” turned into a full campaign victory lap.

Then I did the totally reasonable thing and played the entire game again. This time my mission was domination of the leaderboards. I replayed every level trying to grab first place and managed to claim the top spot on all but one stage. There is always that one level sitting there like a smug little drone factory reminding me I am not perfect.

What really sold the experience for me is the ability to become the turret. Instead of just placing defenses and watching the chaos, you can jump into a turret in first-person and personally blast incoming drones. Not only is it ridiculously fun, it also boosts the turret’s damage by 50%, which basically turns you into the overcaffeinated supervisor of your own defense system.

If you enjoy tower defense games that quietly steal hours of your life while you whisper “just one more round,” Strike Command absolutely delivers. Just don’t blame me when you look up and realize you’ve finished the game twice and are arguing with a leaderboard at 2 AM. 🎮🚀

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for loving the game. I also noticed that you’re almost always in first place on the leaderboard. It looks like you’ve really figured out the strategy. Congratulations, and great job!

reviews.review_description:
Tower Defense games are not much produced in VR, and it's a shame.
I really like this kind of games, and i don't really know, but when i just want to play for a little moment.. well, my battery fall off before me :D

This one is a good one, just with what we want and need in a Tower Defense...
Oh !! I'm lying : this game have the little thing which makes the difference as you can directly go in a turret to destroy yourself all these invaders.
I like and recommand !

developer_response.body: I’m really happy you loved the game! Thanks for playing. If you have any suggestions or questions, I’d love to hear them.

reviews.review_description:
Very nice game and for a great price.

Starts off simple, quickly picks up the pace.

Place your defences, getting kills gives you coins to buy more defences… three entry points for enemies, cover them with your towers of defence.
Learn the order of attacks and then speed up the game, to try beat the times on the leader board.. or beat your friends :)

Graphics are decent… addictive game play.

As you progress, you also unlock better units to use in your defences.

It’s a great game at a great price 👍🏻

developer_response.body: I appreciate you trying out the game! It’s great to hear you enjoyed it. If you have any suggestions or questions, I’d love to hear them.

reviews.review_description: It’s easy to understand and possibly best suited for shorter play sessions. Overall, Strike Command is a solid, accessible VR tower-defence experience that blends strategy with hands-on action. probably best suited for players who enjoy wave-based defence gameplay with light tactical elements. I am enjoying this game, with much more to learn

developer_response.body: I’m so glad you enjoyed the game! Thank you for playing. If you have any suggestions or questions, I’m all ears.

reviews.review_description:
I’ve played enough RTS games to know most of them feel flat, especially in VR. This one doesn’t. The moment you jump in, you’re not just giving orders, you’re actually there. And when you grab control of that turret yourself… yeah, that’s the moment it clicks. It’s just pure chaos in the best way.
Everything feels tight. Controls make sense instantly, no fighting with menus or clunky mechanics. The battles keep pulling you in, and there’s enough strategy going on without it ever turning into a slow, boring grind.
This is one of those games where you say “just one more round” and suddenly an hour’s gone. If you’ve been waiting for a VR RTS that actually delivers instead of feeling like a demo… this is it.

developer_response.body: Thanks for trying the game! I’m glad to hear you loved it.

reviews.review_description:
To start off the graphics are pretty good, I don’t always understand these types of games, but this one is fairly easy to jump into , set up your defence and let them do their thing, it ramps up , but plenty to enjoy here.
The action is on point and sound is very satisfying.
One of those games that you never knew you needed.
Well done to another successful game from the dev.

developer_response.body: Thank you for giving the game a try! I’m happy to hear you loved it. If you have any thoughts or questions, just let me know.

reviews.review_title: A New Soul for Classic Tower Defense
reviews.review_description:
A fresh soul breathes life into classic tower defense.
From a godlike, top-down view, you place turrets to hold back wave after wave — just like the good old days.

But when the battle heats up, you can step inside a turret and fight on the front lines yourself, turning the game into a first-person shooter in an instant.

It feels like an arcade dream from the past, reborn with a bold new twist — creative, nostalgic, and impossible to put down.

developer_response.body: I’m so happy you enjoyed the game! Thanks for giving it a go. If you have any feedback or questions, don’t hesitate to reach out.

reviews.review_description: I really like tower defense games, so I was super excited to play such an awesome one on Quest. I think the coolest part is that you can aim and shoot the enemies by yourself. That’s just amazing!

developer_response.body: I’m thrilled you enjoyed the game! Thank you for playing. If you have any questions or ideas, feel free to let me know.

reviews.review_description:
Defend your tower by placing machine to destroys the alien machines. 

3 different modes with 20 stages. So, you can play 60 different stages.

developer_response.body: I’m so glad you enjoyed the game! Thank you for playing.

reviews.review_description: … it’s 6 am where I am and just finished playing. Awesome game.

developer_response.body: I’m thrilled you enjoyed the game! Thank you for playing.

reviews.review_description: Its good

developer_response.body: I’m so happy you enjoyed the game!

reviews.review_description:
Strike Command VR is one of those games that quickly pulls you in and refuses to let go. At first glance it might look like a straightforward VR action experience, but once you start playing, the depth of its strategy system becomes clear and that’s where the game really shines.

The strategic gameplay is the heart of Strike Command. Instead of just reacting to enemies, you have to constantly plan ahead placing defenses, managing resources and upgrades, and deciding how to deal with incoming waves. The game blends tower-defense style mechanics with hands-on VR combat, meaning you’re not just watching your strategy unfold—you’re actively participating in it by selecting the target icon! Players build defensive structures, guide enemy paths, and jump into the action to support their defenses when things get intense.  

What makes the strategy system so satisfying is how flexible it is. Every wave feels like a small puzzle should you invest in stronger defenses, spread out your structures, or rely on your own weapons to handle the pressure? The game constantly pushes you to adapt and rethink your tactics, which keeps every session fresh.

But the real secret of Strike Command is how addictive it becomes. Each run encourages you to try “just one more wave” or experiment with a slightly different strategy. When a plan works perfectly and you hold off a massive enemy assault, it feels incredibly rewarding. And when things go wrong, you immediately start thinking about what you could do differently next time.

The immersive VR perspective makes the experience even more engaging. Standing in the middle of your battlefield while managing defenses and fighting enemies yourself creates a level of involvement that traditional strategy games can’t match.

Strike Command VR delivers a clever blend of strategy and action that feels perfectly suited for virtual reality. Its tactical depth keeps your brain engaged, while the fast paced gameplay makes it dangerously addictive. If you enjoy strategic planning mixed with hands-on combat, this game is definitely worth trying and adding to your library.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for trying out the game! I'm thrilled that you enjoyed it. Thank you very much for your detailed review as well. If you have any questions or feedback, don’t hesitate to reach out.
